summary: Full 樓層模式 (floors tracking) flow verified end-to-end against live Supabase. 老總(general_foreman member) created a floors leaf in 2座(B) under parent 01 (code 01-01, 4 floor labels) and assigned it to 管工(60001004). 管工 ticked 1樓+2樓 via the exact updateFloors PATCH: actual_progress became 50 (= floorsToProgress 2/4) with status in-progress (= deriveStatus(50,0)), and the progress_history row recorded floors_completed=[1樓,2樓] (v34 assignee-history path works). PM read the item fully consistent via get_visible_progress_items. The case's expected DELETE denial did NOT occur — but that is correct, not a bug: 管工's per-project membership role is main_contractor, which is in the v27 supervisor allowlist, and the client's canManageStructure uses the identical allowlist, so client and DB agree that this member may delete; the delete cascade-removed history cleanly with no orphans.

bugs.title: 管工 (foreman) deletes a progress item carrying live progress + audit history with no confirmation — by design, but high blast-radius for the colloquial 'foreman' label
bugs.repro: Sign in as 60001004 (黃管工, global_role=main_contractor, sub_role=foreman, membership role main_contractor in cccc2026). DELETE /rest/v1/progress_items?id=eq.<floors leaf the foreman was merely assigned to, that already has actual_progress=50 and a progress_history row>. Result: HTTP 200, item deleted, its progress_history cascade-deleted (0 rows remain).
bugs.evidence: DELETE returned 200 with the deleted row. v27 can_manage_project_progress allowlist = ('pm','general_foreman','main_contractor') by MEMBERSHIP role; 黃管工's membership = main_contractor so it qualifies. Client ProgressContext.canManageStructure (src/contexts/ProgressContext.tsx L64-76) uses the SAME allowlist, so the UI shows the same delete affordance — client and DB are aligned (NOT a mismatch). Concern is only that a person titled 管工/foreman can hard-delete an item they were assigned (not one they built) including its dispute audit trail, with no confirm step surfaced server-side. Whether 管工s should be enrolled as main_contractor members is a seeding/role-assignment policy question, not a code defect.

notes: Case expectation correction: the prompt said 管工 DELETE should be denied (supervisor-only). On this seed it is ALLOWED and that is correct — 60001004's MEMBERSHIP role is main_contractor (a supervisor tier per v27), not subcontractor/worker. A true denial test needs a contributor whose membership role is subcontractor/subcontractor_worker/owner (e.g. 何判頭 60001005, membership subcontractor) — that path would 0-row/42501 deny. All positive assertions passed: floors create, assign, contributor updateFloors (actual 50 = floorsToProgress 2/4, status in-progress = deriveStatus(50,0)), progress_history floors_completed recorded (v34 assignee path), PM full-tree read consistency, clean FK cascade on delete (no orphaned history). Verified profile: 60001004 global_role=main_contractor sub_role=foreman. Test artifact self-cleaned (item deleted in step 5; history cascade-deleted). Write path note: Bash/MSYS curl --data-binary @file gave libcurl err 43/HTTP000 on this Windows box; used PowerShell Invoke-RestMethod with UTF-8 bytes for all writes (UTF-8 Chinese round-tripped correctly).
